mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS stays near 0.91 and CPIAUCSL remains elevated, markets read policy as persistently accommodative relative to inflation. That combination lifts inflation expectations and term premium, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: early tightening normalization after a low-rate regime
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward Treasury yield changes are negative in most instances when FEDFUNDS is low and CPIAUCSL is rising, or if the sign flips after controlling for CPIAUCSL trend.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 4.3% signals labor-market slack and weaker wage pressure. That reduces expected inflation and policy tightening risk, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp from the prior monthly print
failure_condition: Rejected if yield changes are positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero after UNRATE threshold events, or if the yield does not fall in at least 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales and housing activity together indicate demand resilience that supports growth expectations and nominal rates. When RSAFS and HOUST both strengthen, the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both post month-over-month gains
failure_condition: Rejected if joint RSAFS-HOUST improvements do not precede higher yields, or if the effect disappears when only one of the two series is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Improving industrial production signals stronger real activity and tighter resource utilization. If INDPRO rises persistently from its latest 105.0284 reading, investors demand a higher nominal yield on the 10-year Treasury over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Rejected if two-month INDPRO improvements are not followed by positive 20-day yield changes, or if the average yield response is negative across historical trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: A weaker GDPC1 print relative to prior quarters signals slowing aggregate demand and softer inflation pressure. In that growth-downshift regime, the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: growth slowdown with decelerating real output
failure_condition: Rejected if GDPC1 slowdowns are not associated with lower forward yields over 60 trading days, or if the effect is absent in the most recent two GDP downshift episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned near 1%, real policy rates turn more negative and markets price a sharper future tightening path. That repricing lifts the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month moving average
regime_hint: inflation surprise during low policy rates
failure_condition: Rejected if inflation-upsurge events with low FEDFUNDS do not produce a short-horizon yield pop, or if the 5-day sign is negative in most triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration that weakens growth and inflation expectations. Over a 20-day horizon this should push the 10-year Treasury yield lower.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release
failure_condition: Rejected if the combined labor softening signal does not precede lower yields, or if yield declines are not more frequent than 50% after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below prior levels while retail sales stay firm implies demand rotation away from rate-sensitive sectors without a broad collapse in spending. In that mixed regime, the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 60 trading days because growth remains intact and inflation risk stays sticky.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for two consecutive months while RSAFS remains above its 3-month average
failure_condition: Rejected if the mixed housing-sales regime is not followed by higher yields over 60 trading days, or if the effect disappears when RSAFS is not above its 3-month average.
